prerequisites: A running SingleStore deployment (SingleStore Helios workspace or a self-managed cluster) reachable from where pm runs.
The host (endpoint) and port of the deployment. SingleStore listens on the MySQL protocol with a default port of 3306.
The name of the database to read from (the `database` config field).
A database user (`username`) and its `password`. The user must already exist; create it with CREATE USER, since auto-creation via GRANT is disabled by default on Helios.
SELECT privileges for the user on the target database, for example GRANT SELECT ON mydb.* TO 'user'@'%'.
A choice of `replication_method` for how pm reads changes from the source.
If connecting over TLS/SSL: the CA certificate that signed the server certificate. For mutual TLS, also a client certificate and client key (and the key password if the key is encrypted).

authMethods.name: Username and password
authMethods.summary: Standard database authentication. Provide the `username` config field and the corresponding `password` secret. The user is created with CREATE USER and granted SELECT on the target database; SingleStore speaks the MySQL protocol, so connections may require the mysql_native_password authentication plugin.

authMethods.name: TLS/SSL (with optional mutual TLS)
authMethods.summary: Configured via the optional `ssl_mode` field. Supply `ssl_mode.ca_certificate` (the CA bundle, e.g. singlestore_bundle.pem) to encrypt and verify the server certificate. For certificate-based mutual TLS, additionally provide `ssl_mode.client_certificate`, `ssl_mode.client_key`, and `ssl_mode.client_key_password`; the matching CA must be trusted by the SingleStore workspace and the user configured with REQUIRE X509.

setupSteps.title: Locate your endpoint and port
setupSteps.body: From your SingleStore Helios workspace (or self-managed cluster), find the connection endpoint. Use the host as the `host` config field and the port as `port`. The default SingleStore port is 3306.

setupSteps.title: Create a database user
setupSteps.body: Connect as an administrative user and run CREATE USER to add a dedicated user for pm, for example CREATE USER 'pm_user'@'%' IDENTIFIED BY '<password>'. SingleStore disables auto user creation via GRANT (NO_AUTO_CREATE_USER is on by default), so the user must be created explicitly.

setupSteps.title: Grant read access
setupSteps.body: Grant the user SELECT on the database you want to sync, for example GRANT SELECT ON mydb.* TO 'pm_user'@'%'. New users have no privileges by default. Scope the grant to the specific database (or table) you intend to read.

setupSteps.title: Set credentials and database
setupSteps.body: Provide the `username` and `password` for the user you created, and set `database` to the database name. These are the minimum credentials for a non-TLS connection.

setupSteps.title: (Optional) Configure TLS/SSL
setupSteps.body: To encrypt the connection, populate `ssl_mode` with the CA certificate (`ssl_mode.ca_certificate`), downloading SingleStore's CA bundle (singlestore_bundle.pem) if needed. For mutual TLS, also provide `ssl_mode.client_certificate`, `ssl_mode.client_key`, and, if the key is encrypted, `ssl_mode.client_key_password`; ensure the signing CA is uploaded to the workspace Security tab and the user requires X509.

setupSteps.title: (Optional) Add JDBC parameters and pick a replication method
setupSteps.body: Use `jdbc_url_params` to append extra connection parameters if your environment needs them (for example to force the mysql_native_password auth plugin). Set the required `replication_method` to control how pm reads data from the source.
